channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Majority of Indian births in 2024 were first children with recommended birth spacing of 36 months
item.description: According to the 2024 Sample Registration System (SRS) Statistical Report, nearly two-thirds (66.4%) of live births in India were first-born children. Fourth-order and higher births accounted for only...

item.title: Newly Discovered Patagonian Raptor Suggests Heron-Like Fishing Behavior in Late Cretaceous Dinosaurs
item.description: Paleontologists have identified a new species of raptor-like dinosaur, named Kank australis, that lived approximately 70 million years ago in what is now southern Patagonia, Argentina. The discovery i...

item.title: Stakeholders Call for Better Standards and Safety in Digital Transportation Services
item.description: Transport service providers na wananchi wanaona kuna haja ya kuboresha huduma za digital transport. Wanaona kuna changamoto nyingi kama ubora wa huduma kutokuwepo kwa uhakika, usalama wa abiria na mad...

item.title: U.S. Military Anti-Drug Operations Show Little Impact on Cocaine Supply, Experts Say
item.description: A recent report highlighted by The New York Times examines the effectiveness of U.S. military operations targeting drug trafficking routes in the eastern Pacific under former President Donald Trump’s ...

item.title: Trump endorses Mike Mazzei for Oklahoma governor, surprising local Republican insiders
item.description: Former President Donald Trump issued a high-profile endorsement in Oklahoma on Friday, backing Mike Mazzei in the state’s upcoming governor’s race. In a Truth Social post, Trump praised Mazzei as a “M...

item.title: Nigeria sends 13-member squad to 2026 African Taekwondo Championships in Mali
item.description: A 13-member Team Nigeria contingent has officially begun its participation at the 2026 African Taekwondo Championships holding in Bamako, Mali. The squad includes both Kyorugi fighters and, for the fi...

item.title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth Faces Criticism for Speech to Navy Sailors in Singapore
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth delivered a motivational speech to Navy personnel aboard the USS Boxer while the ship was docked in Singapore. The address was intended to inspire sailors involved ...

item.title: Marc Rudolph: Life and Career of Maya Rudolph’s Musician Brother
item.description: Marc Rudolph is an American musician and music engineer, born in 1968 in Gainesville, Florida, as the only son of legendary soul singer Minnie Riperton and music producer Richard Rudolph. Despite grow...

item.title: Key considerations before signing a vehicle finance agreement
item.description: Before signing a vehicle finance agreement (VAF), South African motorists are advised to fully understand the long-term financial implications beyond simply securing approval. While approval from a le...

item.title: Viral childhood photo leads Toy Story fan to attend UK premiere and meet film stars
item.description: Vinny Donnelly, who first captured global attention as a young child, has experienced an unexpected return to the spotlight after being invited to the UK premiere of the latest Toy Story film. Six yea...

item.title: South Africa’s property market faces structural divide between affordable and middle-to-upper housing segments
item.description: South Africa’s residential property sector is increasingly characterised by structural imbalances, with a clear divide between a strong-performing middle-to-upper housing market and a constrained, und...

item.title: A Collection of Early 20th Century Good Housekeeping Magazine Covers (1910–1919)
item.description: This article presents a curated selection of vintage covers from Good Housekeeping magazine spanning the 1910s, highlighting the publication’s artistic and cultural significance during the early 20th ...

item.title: Man Hospitalised After Hanging from Moving Van on West Gate Bridge, Melbourne
item.description: On Friday afternoon, peak-hour traffic on Melbourne’s West Gate Bridge turned chaotic after a man was filmed clinging to the windscreen of a moving van on the M1 West Gate Freeway around 5pm. The man ...

item.title: Four arrested in Vancouver following alleged $200,000 casino cheating investigation
item.description: Four individuals—two men and two women—were arrested in Vancouver following an investigation into an alleged casino cheating operation involving more than $200,000. According to authorities, suspected...

item.title: Black bear euthanized in Golden Ears Park after repeated human conflict incidents
item.description: A female black bear was euthanized by the B.C. Conservation Officer Service in Golden Ears Provincial Park near Maple Ridge after exhibiting increasingly aggressive behaviour and repeated conflicts wi...

item.title: Eleven-year-old boy rescued from burning storage container; 13-year-old arrested
item.description: An 11-year-old boy was rescued from a burning storage container at Trimsaran Rugby Club in Carmarthenshire, South Wales, thanks to the quick actions of several local fathers. The group, who were enjoy...

item.title: Jury deliberates in Alberta highway shooting murder trial involving two accused men
item.description: Jurors have begun deliberations in the second-degree murder trial of two men accused of fatally shooting a stranger on a southern Alberta highway after a chaotic roadside incident involving a burning ...

item.title: Why the Philippines is pushing for algorithmic and platform accountability in social media regulation
item.description: The article discusses the growing legislative effort in the Philippines to address disinformation by focusing not only on individual users, but also on the algorithms and platforms that shape online c...

item.title: India-Russia discuss Arctic trade corridor and defence cooperation
item.description: National Security Advisor Ajit Doval held talks with Nikolai Patrushev, the Russian President’s aide and chairman of Russia’s Maritime Board, in Moscow on May 29. The discussions reviewed progress on ...

item.title: White House Considers Ending Trump’s Controversial Legal Fund Amid GOP Concerns
item.description: White House officials are reportedly evaluating whether to discontinue President Donald Trump's $1.776 billion 'Anti-Weaponization Fund,' a legal settlement widely criticized as a slush fund for allie...
